items.definition_en: Waste collection is the process of gathering garbage or recyclables from homes or businesses.
items.example_en: The waste collection happens every Monday in our neighborhood.

items.definition_en: A landfill is a place where waste is buried under the ground.
items.example_en: Most of our city’s garbage ends up in a landfill.

items.definition_en: Recycling is the process of turning waste into new products.
items.example_en: Recycling helps reduce the amount of trash we produce.

items.definition_en: A waste audit is an analysis to find out what kinds of waste are being thrown away.
items.example_en: The company ordered a waste audit to find ways to reduce trash.

items.definition_en: Hazardous waste is dangerous trash like chemicals or batteries.
items.example_en: Hazardous waste cannot be disposed of with regular garbage.

items.definition_en: Organic waste is biodegradable trash like food scraps or garden clippings.
items.example_en: Most of our organic waste goes to composting facilities.

items.definition_en: Composting is the process of turning organic waste into soil.
items.example_en: Our office started composting to lower its environmental impact.

items.definition_en: Incineration is burning waste at high temperatures to reduce its volume.
items.example_en: Incineration helps to cut down on the amount of waste sent to landfills.

items.definition_en: A recycling facility is a place where waste materials are processed for reuse.
items.example_en: The recycling facility processes plastics, glass, and paper every day.

items.definition_en: A dumpster is a large container for holding waste until it is collected.
items.example_en: They threw old furniture into the dumpster behind the store.

items.definition_en: Scrap is leftover metal or other materials that can often be recycled.
items.example_en: The factory sells its scrap to metal recycling companies.

items.definition_en: A collection route is the planned path taken by waste collection trucks.
items.example_en: The city changed the collection route to make pickups more efficient.

items.definition_en: Waste segregation is separating trash into categories like paper, plastic, and glass.
items.example_en: Proper waste segregation helps increase recycling rates.

items.definition_en: A disposal fee is the money paid to get rid of waste.
items.example_en: The new disposal fee made some people recycle more.

items.definition_en: Industrial waste is trash produced by factories and businesses.
items.example_en: The factory must follow strict rules for industrial waste.

items.definition_en: Municipal solid waste is everyday trash from homes and businesses.
items.example_en: Municipal solid waste collection is a vital city service.

items.definition_en: A transfer station is a site where waste is temporarily stored before being transported.
items.example_en: Trucks take garbage from the city to the transfer station.

items.definition_en: A sanitation worker is a person who collects and manages waste.
items.example_en: The sanitation worker arrives before dawn to pick up trash.

items.definition_en: Bulky waste is large items like furniture or appliances that cannot be recycled.
items.example_en: Bulky waste must be scheduled for pickup by the city.
